scheme_name: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Stationary-Text Books
page_title: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Stationary-Text Books

details: The scheme “Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Stationary-Text Books” by the Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu, is designed to support students from 1st to 12th standard who are enrolled in Adi Dravidar and Tribal welfare schools by offering them free textbooks. This initiative aims to ensure that no student is deprived of education due to a lack of study materials.
benefits: Free textbooks for students from 1st to 12th standard.
eligibility: The beneficiary should be a Student.
The beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu.
The beneficiary should be enrolled in Adi Dravidar and Tribal welfare schools.
The beneficiary must be studying in any class between 1st to 12th standard.

offline: Students/ Guardians should contact the Headmaster of the School with all the required documents as directed by the authority.

documents_required: Proof of identity (Aadhar card or any other document as directed by the authority).
Proof of enrollment in Adi Dravidar and Tribal welfare school.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the scheme?
faqs.answer: This scheme provides free textbooks to students from 1st to 12th standard in Adi Dravidar and Tribal welfare schools.

faqs.question: Who qualifies for free textbooks under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Students enrolled in Adi Dravidar and Tribal welfare schools from 1st to 12th standard.

faqs.question: What are the key benefits of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides free textbooks to eligible students.

faqs.question: How can students access the free textbooks?
faqs.answer: Textbooks are distributed through the school’s headmaster.

faqs.question: Is there any income eligibility for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there are no income-based restrictions for eligibility.

faqs.question: What documentation is required to benefit from the scheme?
faqs.answer: Proof of enrollment of the school along with other documents is necessary.

faqs.question: Can students from private or other schools apply for the textbooks?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is exclusively for students in Adi Dravidar and Tribal welfare schools.

faqs.question: Is this scheme available to students of all grade levels?
faqs.answer: Yes, it covers students from 1st to 12th standard.

faqs.question: When are the textbooks made available?
faqs.answer: Textbooks are usually distributed at the start of the academic year.

faqs.question: Is there a specific time frame to receive the textbooks?
faqs.answer: The textbooks are available throughout the academic year.

faqs.question: Are students from non-Adi Dravidar or non-Tribal backgrounds eligible?
faqs.answer: No, only students attending Adi Dravidar and Tribal welfare schools are eligible.

faqs.question: What role does the headmaster play in the scheme?
faqs.answer: The headmaster is responsible for distributing textbooks to the eligible students.
